channel.title: Pablo Bernabeu
channel.description: Recent content in linguistic relativity on Pablo Bernabeu
channel.copyright: Pablo Bernabeu, 2015—{year}. Licence: [CC BY 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/). Email: pcbernabeu@gmail.com. Cookies only used by third-party systems such as [Disqus](https://help.disqus.com/en/articles/1717155-use-of-cookies).

item.title: More refined typology and design in linguistic relativity: The case of motion event encoding
item.description:
Study at a glance

graph TD A["Linguistic relativity:
language influences cognition"] --> B["Motion event encoding
(MEE) typology"] B --> C["Path-in-verb languages
(e.g., leave)"] B --> D["Manner-in-verb languages
(e.g., jump)"] A --> E["Mixed prior results: no effect
to verbal and nonverbal effects"] E --> F["Proposed enhancements"] F --> G["Refined typology across twenty
languages (Verkerk, 2014)"] F --> H["Distinguish verbal and nonverbal
subgroups, add filler items"] G --> I["Improved experimental design"] H --> I

Reference

Bernabeu, P., & Tillman, R. (2019). More refined typology and design in linguistic relativity: The case of motion event encoding. Dutch Journal of Applied Linguistics, 8(2), 163-171. http://doi.org/10.1075/dujal.15019.ber
